response:
source
The initial setup requires defining variables to hold the sum of squares and the product of cubes within the function scope.
***
We initialize the sum variable to zero and the product variable to one to handle empty lists correctly as specified.
***
A loop iterates through each integer in the provided slice to calculate the necessary mathematical transformations for every element.
***
Inside the loop we add the square of the current number to our running sum and multiply the cube of the number into our product.
***
After processing all items the function returns the final calculated values as an ordered pair in the required array format.
***
This implementation ensures that an empty input results in zero for the sum and one for the product while correctly handling non-empty sets.
